articles.title: Get ready for Google I/O 2026
articles.snippet: Google I/O returns May 19-20. Watch the livestreams for updates on Android, AI, Chrome, and Cloud. Registration is open on the Google I/O website.
articles.ai_summary: Google I/O 2026 is scheduled for May 19-20, with livestreams covering updates on Android, AI, Chrome, and Cloud. Registration is now open.
articles.why_it_matters: Anticipate major announcements in AI and tech from one of the industry's leading events.

articles.title: Turn creative prompts into interactive XR experiences with Gemini
articles.snippet: The Android XR team is using Gemini's Canvas feature to make creating immersive extended reality (XR) experiences more accessible. This allows developers to rapidly prototype interactive 3D environments and models on a Samsung Galaxy XR headset using simple creative prompts.
articles.ai_summary: The Android XR team is leveraging Gemini's Canvas feature to simplify the creation of interactive XR experiences, allowing rapid prototyping on Samsung Galaxy XR headsets.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances accessibility for developers in creating immersive experiences, pushing the boundaries of XR technology.

articles.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
articles.snippet: Google has introduced FunctionGemma, a specialized 270M parameter model designed to bring efficient, action-oriented AI experiences directly to mobile devices through on-device function calling. By leveraging Google AI Edge and LiteRT-LM, the model enables complex tasks—such as managing calendars, controlling device hardware, or executing specific game logic in the "Tiny Garden" demo—to be performed entirely offline with high speed and low latency. Available for testing in the Google AI Edge Gal
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient AI tasks offline on mobile devices.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers mobile applications with advanced AI capabilities, enhancing user experience without reliance on internet connectivity.

articles.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
articles.snippet: Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports a robust ecosystem of third-party tools and integrations. Connect your agents to GitHub, Notion, Hugging Face, and more to build capable, real-world applications.
articles.ai_summary: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now includes a variety of third-party tool integrations, allowing developers to enhance their AI agents with real-world applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Expands the capabilities of AI agents, fostering innovation and collaboration in application development.

articles.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
articles.snippet: Google I/O 2026 is returning May 19-20 at Shoreline Amphitheatre in Mountain View, CA. But before the keynotes begin, you can get into the spirit of the event with our annual tradition: the save the date puzzle.  This year's experience highlights how AI can empower and accelerate
articles.ai_summary: Google I/O 2026 will feature a 'save the date' puzzle experience that showcases how AI can enhance event engagement before the main event.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights Google's commitment to integrating AI into user experiences, setting the stage for future innovations.

articles.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
articles.snippet: While keynotes are available online, Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas offers an irreplaceable in-person experience centered on networking, hands-on problem solving, and the transition to agentic AI. The event features specialized technical tracks covering everything from Gemini multimodal breakthroughs to zero-trust security on Cloud Run, providing developers with the tools to balance individual speed with organizational stability. Beyond formal sessions, the "in-person advantage" lies in over
articles.ai_summary: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas emphasizes in-person networking and hands-on problem solving, focusing on agentic AI and technical advancements.
articles.why_it_matters: Offers unique opportunities for developers to engage directly with cutting-edge AI technologies and solutions.

articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: TensorFlow 2.21 introduces LiteRT, a new framework for faster GPU and NPU acceleration, enhancing support for GenAI and on-device inference.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes AI deployment efficiency, making it easier for developers to implement advanced models.

articles.title: SharePoint: Extending AI in SharePoint using custom skills
articles.snippet: Skills allow extensibility of the SharePoint AI Agent, enabling customizable solutions that adapt to unique business needs. They address gaps in out-of-the-box functionality for unique business processes and enable reusable multi-step workflows. GA date: May CY2026Preview date: April CY2026
articles.ai_summary: SharePoint is enhancing its AI capabilities with customizable skills that allow businesses to tailor solutions to their specific needs.
articles.why_it_matters: Facilitates personalized workflows in SharePoint, improving productivity and adaptability for businesses.

articles.title: Microsoft Teams: Improved organization for muted and meeting chats
articles.snippet: Microsoft Teams automatically organizes your chats with new built in sections that help reduce clutter and keep conversations easy to find. Muted chats (on by default) are grouped together in a dedicated area, while meeting chats (off by default) can be collected into a single section for quick access. You’re always in control, as each section can be turned on or off based on your preferences.GA date: May CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Teams is introducing new chat organization features that group muted and meeting chats, streamlining user experience.
articles.why_it_matters: Improves communication efficiency in Teams, helping users manage conversations more effectively.

articles.title: Microsoft Teams: Facilitator detects and answers questions
articles.snippet: Facilitator now automatically detects open questions raised during a Teams meeting and offers to help answer them using web search. When a participant asks a question (e.g., “What is an LLM?”) and no one answers, Facilitator posts a prompt offering to find an answer. Participants can select “Yes” to have the agent retrieve and share the information.GA date: June CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Teams' new Facilitator feature automatically detects unanswered questions during meetings and offers to find answers via web search. This aims to improve information flow in discussions.

articles.title: Microsoft Teams: Badging updates help find messages that count in the chat list
articles.snippet: Microsoft Teams displays a badge on the app bar that reflects activity across chats and channels. Now, it’s easier to find and clear the messages behind that badge. Unmuted chats show a purple indicator when they affect the badge, while mentions, followed threads, and tag mentions display a purple number indicating how many items within are unread and part of the count.GA date: May CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Teams has introduced badge updates that help users identify important messages in chats and channels more easily. The new features include visual indicators for unread messages and mentions.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances user experience by improving message visibility in Teams.

articles.title: Microsoft Teams: Quick access to read items from unread-only view
articles.snippet: Teams chat and channel lists can be filtered to show unread items only. When this filter is applied, hovering over a section reveals an eye icon next to the section name. Selecting the eye icon opens a list of read chats or channels within that section, allowing you to open them without leaving the unread-only view.GA date: May CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Teams has added a feature that allows users to quickly access read items while filtering for unread messages. This improvement streamlines navigation within the app.
articles.why_it_matters: Streamlines user navigation, improving productivity in Teams.

articles.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention - Apply default SharePoint library labels to data at rest
articles.snippet: Introduce an AI-powered capability leveraging Microsoft Security Copilot to automatically generate natural language explanations of changes made to DLP policies after each update within Microsoft Purview. This feature will analyze policy version deltas (pre-update vs. post-update) and produce a structured, human-readable summary that clearly explains: What changed Where the change occurred (rule, condition, action, location, etc.) The impact of the change The potential enforcement implications T
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Purview has introduced an AI-powered feature that generates natural language summaries of changes made to Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies, improving clarity and understanding of policy updates.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances transparency and understanding of data protection policies.

articles.title: Microsoft Purview: Information Protection - Apply default SharePoint library labels to data at rest
articles.snippet: Enables auto‑labeling of existing SharePoint files to match the default sensitivity label configured on their document library. This helps organizations close labeling gaps for data at rest and ensure consistent, secure‑by‑default protection across SharePoint libraries—without relying on manual user labeling.GA date: May CY2026Preview date: March CY2026
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft Purview's new feature allows for automatic labeling of SharePoint files according to default sensitivity labels, ensuring consistent data protection without manual intervention.
articles.why_it_matters: Automates data protection processes, reducing manual errors and improving security.

articles.title: WhatsApp malware campaign delivers VBS payloads and MSI backdoors
articles.snippet:
A malware campaign uses WhatsApp messages to deliver VBS scripts that initiate a multi-stage infection chain. The attack leverages renamed Windows tools and cloud-hosted payloads to install MSI backdoors and maintain persistent access to compromised systems.
…
